mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and corporate earnings deterioration, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 70 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent, or the signal loses predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal income and rising consumer prices indicate a demand shock that erodes purchasing power and corporate revenue, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when RSAFS falls and CPIAUCSL rises, with a negative Rank IC over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals expanding manufacturing capacity and rising corporate output, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week exclusively during early-cycle recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns during post-shock recovery periods, or the predictive coefficient turns negative in regime-filtered samples.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects strengthening economic activity and rising wage expenditures, which directly supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple market cycles, with a mean Rank IC below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid acceleration in the consumer price index triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which increases discount rates and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns remain positive or flat when CPIAUCSL accelerates past the threshold, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in housing starts and real GDP growth confirms broad-based economic strength and rising asset valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative or zero forward returns when both series expand, or interaction terms lose statistical significance in multivariate regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during late-cycle peaks signals capacity constraints and impending earnings revisions, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to predict negative 5-day returns during late-cycle peaks, or the signal reverses direction in regime-specific backtests.
